properties.name: title
properties.value: ERC-8004: On-Chain Trust for AI Agents

stage.title: Module 4: AI Agent Economy & The Future

concepts.title: AINFT: Autonomous Intelligent NFTs
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- AINFT = NFT + AI logic + dynamic metadata that grows and evolves over time
- Dynamic metadata: specification (AI model params), history (interactions), properties (current state)
- Three core properties: accountable (on-chain record), reproducible (open protocol stack), valuable (component-level rewards)
- AINFT Factory: community API platform for building AINFT tokenomics
- Ownership redefined: not owning a file, but nurturing a growing relationship

**Common Computer (2024)** Chapter 10 evolves the NFT into a 'living entity.'

If a classic NFT is a **photograph**, an AINFT is a **pet**. A photograph is frozen at the moment it's taken, but a pet grows and changes through interactions with its owner. AINFT metadata consists of three parts: specification (AI model parameters), history (interaction records), and properties (current state — level, credits, achievements).

The three core properties of AINFTs are: (1) **accountability** — every relationship in the AI pipeline is immutably recorded on-chain. (2) **reproducibility** — the entire protocol stack is open, making reproduction possible at any time. (3) **value** — long pipelines are decomposed into small components, rewarding individual contributors.

**AINFT Factory** is a shared API platform where communities can build AINFT tokenomics. The MiniEggs project is a prime example — every moment in the metaverse (giving hearts, singing lullabies) becomes AI growth data that shapes the character's personality.

The meaning of ownership is also redefined. Classic NFT ownership meant 'rights to a file,' but in AINFT, ownership means **nurturing a growing relationship and building a shared history**.

concepts.title: ERC-8004: On-Chain Trust for AI Agents
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- ERC-8004 = on-chain trust infrastructure for AI agents to discover, verify, and collaborate
- Identity Registry (ERC-721): each agent = one NFT with capabilities, protocols, wallet info
- Reputation Registry: verified feedback with anti-spam cryptographic proofs
- Validation Registry: optimistic verification, zk-proofs, TEE attestation
- Agent economy stack: identity (ERC-8004) → payment (x402) → communication (A2A/MCP) → tools → accounts (ERC-6551)

**Common Computer (2024)** Chapter 11 addresses the most fundamental question of the AI agent economy: **How do you trust an agent you've never met?**

Think of ERC-8004 as a **passport + resume + diploma system for the digital world**. Three registries implement this:

The **Identity Registry** is the passport. Built on ERC-721 (NFT), each agent holds a single NFT containing its capabilities, communication protocols (MCP/A2A), and wallet address. The revolutionary part is that agent identity itself can be traded on OpenSea.

The **Reputation Registry** is the resume. It standardizes post-task reputation feedback and uses cryptographic proofs to verify actual work and payments, preventing spam. As more reviews accumulate, the average stabilizes, making manipulation increasingly difficult.

The **Validation Registry** is the diploma/certification. It supports three verification methods: optimistic verification (passes if unchallenged), zero-knowledge proofs (mathematical proof without revealing the work), and TEE attestation (hardware-level guarantees).

The agent economy stack is: ERC-8004 (identity and trust) → x402 (payments) → A2A/MCP (communication) → MCP (tools) → ERC-6551 (accounts).

concepts.title: OpenClaw: The Autonomous Digital Worker
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- OpenClaw: open-source local AI agent framework — 150K+ GitHub stars, 416K+ monthly npm downloads
- Why local: privacy (no data leaks), persistence (24/7), bot detection bypass
- Skills-based plugin system: markdown-based, community-contributed extensions
- Delegated authority model: user delegates narrow permissions, agent never holds keys (ERC-4337 + ERC-7710)
- Three pillars convergence: AINFT (ownership) + ERC-8004 (trust) + OpenClaw (execution)

**Common Computer (2024)** final Chapter 12 covers the paradigm shift of AI from 'responder' to 'executor.'

Think of OpenClaw as a **digital personal assistant**. If traditional AI was an 'information desk that answers questions,' OpenClaw is a 'personal assistant that goes to the bank, files paperwork, and does your shopping.' It autonomously executes browser actions, API calls, scripts, and on-chain transactions from natural language commands.

The reasons for local execution are critical: (1) **privacy** — wallet information never leaves the device, (2) **persistence** — runs 24/7 regardless of cloud outages, (3) **bot detection bypass** — maintains natural browser sessions.

**CoinFello Skill's** security model is especially important. If the agent holds private keys, a hack means total loss. Instead, it uses a **delegated authority model** (ERC-4337 + ERC-7710): the user delegates only narrow permissions, and signing keys always remain on the user's device. This is the principle of least privilege.

Ultimately, the **three pillars of the agent economy** converge: AINFT (ownership) + ERC-8004 (trust) + OpenClaw (execution). Communities grow AI through AINFTs, establish trust via ERC-8004, and execute real-world tasks through OpenClaw — with revenue flowing back to the community.

quiz.question: What security model does OpenClaw's CoinFello Skill use?
options: The agent directly stores the user's private key
Keys are stored on a TEE (Trusted Execution Environment) server
The user delegates only narrow permissions, and signing keys always remain on the user's device
quiz.correctAnswer: The user delegates only narrow permissions, and signing keys always remain on the user's device
